programme.title: From The Scene For The Scene
programme.desc: Go beyond the music and into the heart of the underground. This collection pairs heavy-hitting videos with exclusive band interviews and reports from metal capitals worldwide. Built by the scene, for the scene.

programme.title: Finnish Metal Special
programme.desc: From the frozen North comes a sound of haunting melodies and raw intensity. Dive into Finland’s finest, where melancholic atmospheres meet the power of the thousand lakes. It’s a masterclass in darkness and ice-cold riffs.

programme.title: The Home of Heavy
programme.desc: Step into the headquarters for everything that shakes the earth. From the pillars of the classics to the pioneers of the new era, this is where the heart of metal beats loudest. This is where you belong.

programme.title: Wishbone Ash - Live in London - 40th Anniversary Concert
programme.desc: Info not available

programme.title: Your Pulse Of Metal
programme.desc: Sync your heartbeat to the complex rhythms of the underground. This collection dives into technical mastery and hidden gems that push your adrenaline. If you’re ready to feel the music in your veins, we’ve got the tracks.

programme.title: For Metalheads Only
programme.desc: Leave the rock anthems at the door—this is 100% pure steel. We’ve dialed the distortion to eleven for a lineup strictly for the heavy-duty fans. No compromises, no fillers, just total sonic devastation.

programme.title: Night Time Metal
programme.desc: The sun is down, the filters are off, and the real chaos begins. Brace yourself for the raw, uncensored side of the scene too heavy for daylight. This is the unfiltered truth of the underground. Adult ears only.

programme.title: Heavy Pumping
programme.desc: Ditch the pre-workout and let the riffs do the heavy lifting. This high-octane blast of speed and power is designed to fuel your gains or ignite a living room mosh pit. High BPMs, crushing drums, and zero excuses.

programme.title: New Rock And Metal Videos For You
programme.desc: Stay ahead of the curve with the freshest drops from the global underground. From world premieres to the latest studio masterpieces, we’ve gathered the newest sonic assaults in one place. If it’s loud and new, it’s here.

programme.title: Flying V
programme.desc: Info not available

programme.title: Wishbone Ash - Live In Hamburg
programme.desc: Ein legendärer Auftritt einer Hardrock-Legende in Hamburg! Diese Aufnahme vereint frisches Material mit zeitlosen Klassikern. Eine musikalische Reise durch mehr als dreißig Jahre Rockgeschichte, die pure Energie und Nostalgie verspricht.

programme.title: Together We Rise
programme.desc: A tribute to the global brotherhood that refuses to fit in. This collection celebrates the unity that turns fans into a tribe. In this pit, no one stands alone—together, we are the loudest force on earth.

programme.title: L'alfabeto di Giorgione S.4
programme.desc: Un modo divertente e diverso di vivere la cucina. Con Giorgione in cattedra l'alfabeto diventa commestibile. Curiosita' e ricette per ogni lettera.

programme.title: Giorgione a casa tua
programme.desc: Monte Faito - Giorgione a Monte Faito a casa dei suoi fans per cucinare insieme ricette tradizionali si', ma con il suo tocco laido e corrotto.

programme.title: Le grandi cantine S3
programme.desc: DOCG Tullum - Tollo e' un’area da sempre vocata alla produzione del vino. Qui la DOCG Tullum tutela una produzione proveniente da uve selezionate di vitigni autoctoni la cui coltivazione risale a oltre mille anni fa.

programme.title: Come è profondo il mare Egadi
programme.desc: Favignana ep3 - La storia della tonnara di Favignana è la storia della famiglia Florio, la storia della pesca del tonno e della sua trasformazione.

programme.title: Sardegna experience
programme.desc: Il viaggio di Luigi Pomata nella sua Sardegna, dall'entroterra al mare un caleidoscopio di sapori e saperi. Luoghi, persone e tradizioni che rendono unica questa isola

programme.title: Beker on tour
programme.desc: Fabrizio Nonis conoscitore, esperto di tutti i tagli della carne e grande appassionato di BBQ viaggia alla scoperta di luoghi e incontra amici con i quali prepara succulente ricette.

programme.title: Parola di chef S10
programme.desc: Dario Fisichella - Il punto di vista di un giovane talento della ristorazione italiana che si racconta svelando le proprie soddisfazioni, difficolta', aspirazioni, in un percorso tra pubblico e privato.

programme.title: Mangio tutto tranne Portogallo
programme.desc: Porto - Giovanni Angelucci, gastronomo viaggiatore, questa volta in Portogallo. Un reportage di gusto e cultura in compagnia di questo coloratissimo paese

programme.title: La sfida circolare
programme.desc: Peperoni - Igles e Alberto Corelli, padre e figlio impegnati in una sfida circolare tra fornelli e ricette

programme.title: Max cucina l'estate
programme.desc: S.1 - Le ricette di Max per l'estate. idee e consigli per piatti veloci, leggeri e gustosi.

programme.title: Giorgione viaggio lungo il Po
programme.desc: Pesci bilancione - Il Taro, affluente del Po e fiume navigabile, porta Giorgione a Gramignazzo, al suo famoso Ponte del Diavolo e alla fornace. Ripartendo lungo il Taro, arriva poi al bilancione, un sistema di pesca tipico di questa zona.

programme.title: Vito con i suoi S1
programme.desc: Tortellini - Stefano Bicocchi, in arte Vito, apre le porte di casa sua e ci fa entrare nella tradizione della cucina bolognese raccontandoci la sua Bologna, fatta di ricordi, profumi e sapori.

programme.title: Le ricette dei food-metti
programme.desc: Metti un fumetto a cena con Cristiano Tomei. Lo chef dedica un piatto a ciascuno dei suoi fumetti preferiti.

programme.title: L'alfabeto di Giorgione S.4
programme.desc: Un modo divertente e diverso di vivere la cucina. Con Giorgione in cattedra l'alfabeto diventa commestibile. Curiosita' e ricette per ogni lettera.

programme.title: Giorgione a casa tua
programme.desc: Monte Faito - Giorgione a Monte Faito a casa dei suoi fans per cucinare insieme ricette tradizionali si', ma con il suo tocco laido e corrotto.

programme.title: Ritratto di chef
programme.desc: S5 - Una cucina semplice, riconoscibile, alla ricerca dell’equilibrio ma anche volta alla ricerca del nuovo, quella di Alfio Ghezzi, espressione di un legame indissolubile con la sua terra

programme.title: Gambero Rosso
programme.desc: Rubrica

programme.title: Il vino in pillole
programme.desc: I grandi vini spiegati da Giuseppe Carrus. I segreti per apprezzarne al meglio le caratteristiche organolettiche e i migliori abbinamenti

programme.title: Friuli Patrimonio Unico
programme.desc: Palu di Livenza - Dal mare ai monti il viaggio di Fabrizio Nonis in Friuli Venezia Giulia. Un patrimonio di bellezza, unicità, natura incontaminata, di luoghi da scoprire e di sapori da gustare

programme.title: Ritratto di chef
programme.desc: S5 - Una cucina semplice, riconoscibile, alla ricerca dell’equilibrio ma anche volta alla ricerca del nuovo, quella di Alfio Ghezzi, espressione di un legame indissolubile con la sua terra

programme.title: Ritratto di chef
programme.desc: S5 - L’avventura italiana di Kobe in Sicilia, uno chef che ha fatto del rapporto con i piccoli produttori la sua filosofia di cucina. Un parlare quotidiano e serrato coi pescatori, con gli agricoltori, con le alghe dei vicini fondali.

programme.title: Ritratto di chef
programme.desc: S5 - L’avventura italiana di Kobe in Sicilia, uno chef che ha fatto del rapporto con i piccoli produttori la sua filosofia di cucina. Un parlare quotidiano e serrato coi pescatori, con gli agricoltori, con le alghe dei vicini fondali.

programme.title: Vino, cibo e natura
programme.desc: S.4 Nella magica cornice di Castello del Terriccio Cristiano Tomei, Giuseppe Carrus e un ospite a sorpresa in una esperienza che unisce vino cibo e natura.

programme.title: Max alla brace
programme.desc: S.1 - Max Mariola in cortile alle prese con un bbq. Ricette gustose e originali da realizzare nel giardino di casa o per passare qualche ora all’aperto

programme.title: Giorgione viaggio lungo il Po
programme.desc: Pesci bilancione - Il Taro, affluente del Po e fiume navigabile, porta Giorgione a Gramignazzo, al suo famoso Ponte del Diavolo e alla fornace. Ripartendo lungo il Taro, arriva poi al bilancione, un sistema di pesca tipico di questa zona.

programme.title: Vito con i suoi e Giorgione
programme.desc: Giorgione in trasferta. La famiglia Bicocchi al completo invita Giorgione a San Giovanni Persiceto per trascorrere qualche giorno in compagnia di ricette tradizionali, laide, corrotte e test

programme.title: La mia Sardegna
programme.desc: Terra e vento - Terra fertile e generosa, terra di vento che rinfresca e pulisce. Giuseppe si sposta in Marmilla, a Sanluri terra di grano fin dalle antichità. Dove c’è grano c’è vita, e qui c’è un importante progetto di rinascita enogastronomica del territorio.

programme.title: La mia Sardegna
programme.desc: Il tramonto prima dell'alba - Il viaggio parte da Alghero per arrivare a Capo Caccia ad ammirare uno dei tramonti più suggestivi della Sardegna. Durante il viaggio incontrerà amici chef e vignaioli che racconteranno le loro storie e i loro progetti

programme.title: Una pizza in compagnia
programme.desc: La passione per la pizza di Renato Bosco, una passione curiosa e golosa che lo spinge a cercare nuove contaminazioni e nuove sperimentazioni.

programme.title: Hiro in Japan
programme.desc: Il viaggio di Hiro in Giappone, sua terra natia. La cucina, la cultura, le tradizioni e i luoghi di una delle civilta' piu' affascinanti e antiche

programme.title: Gelato e dintorni
programme.desc: Il gelato artigianale è una vera e propria espressione della tradizione gastronomica italiana. Giovanni Angelucci racconta l’importanza di una filiera controllata e certificata nella produzione del gelato artigianale.

programme.title: due chef in vacanza
programme.desc: Davide Carlo e Enrico Corelli due chef professionisti, giovani, belli e finalmente in vacanza...un po' fuori stagione. Mare, sole, amici e tanta buona cucina.

programme.title: Otre il mare S.2
programme.desc: Giuseppe Carrus continua il suo viaggio questa volta alla scoperta delle cantine più importanti e rappresentative della Toscana. Tra colline e vigneti uno sguardo appassionato verso le realtà vitivinicole di questa regione.

programme.title: Odissea nelle spezie
programme.desc: Jinny e Franco due mondi che si incontrano e che hanno in comune una grande passione: le spezie. Cardamomo, zenzero, cannella, cumino, un universo esoterico di colori, aromi, storie e ricette.

programme.title: Con le mani in pasta
programme.desc: Nonna Rina: Nonna Rina, il suo pangiallo e i frittilli di mele e broccoli. Ricette antiche che scaldano il cuore

programme.title: Max alla brace
programme.desc: S.1 - Max Mariola in cortile alle prese con un bbq. Ricette gustose e originali da realizzare nel giardino di casa o per passare qualche ora all’aperto

programme.title: Giorgione viaggio lungo il Po
programme.desc: Luccioperca - In navigazione silenziosa si arriva alla foce del Taro. Qui dopo una breve tappa a Coltaro al museo Cantoni, dove scopre le origini del Concerto Cantoni, un concerto di soli fiati, Giorgione fa tappa in cucina con il luccio.

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Talented baker Jessica McGovern shows us how to get fancy with three stunning French desserts.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: In this "C" challenge, Chef Robert Jewell cooks up recipes using coconut and crab before combining both into a single recipe. Using sweet coconut, Robert makes a classic dessert -- an irresistible coconut cream pie. He starts with a delectable crunchy graham cracker crust and tops his pie with fluffy whipped cream and toasted coconut flakes. Next, Robert transforms a fresh soft-shell crab into a delicious, spicy, and crunchy dish. The best part? It's fast and easy to make. After soaking the crab in milk, Robert dunks it into a flour-cornmeal mixture and fries it until golden brown. But that's not all, Robert whips up the perfect dipping sauce, using spicy smoked- jalapeno, lime, and orange adobo mayo. Finally, Robert brings coconut and crab into one delicious dish.

programme.title: A is for Apple
programme.desc: It's all about sesame and scallops in this letter "S" challenge, and host Theresa Visintin has some appetizing ideas up her sleeve. First, she shows us how to make scallop cassoulet, a simple recipe that can be cooked even faster than it can eaten! Theresa browns the tender scallops in delicious bacon fat and then bakes it on a bed of creamy kidney beans. Next, Theresa uses sesame seeds to create a zesty sauce for a take-out favourite, made in your very own kitchen - noodles! For her final recipe, our culinary host combines sesame and scallops, creating a juicy, crunchy masterpiece.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Join engaging host, Bianca Osbourne, for a taste of two countries. First stop? Italy! Check out her recipe for succulent wild boar ribs, braised until tender, rolled in gremolata, and served over silky polenta. Next up? A culinary tribute to China, pairing lobster claw with chili oil, pomelo foam, and oyster sauce glazed bok choy. Every episode features a 'common ingredient', this time? It's noodles! Drool over two recipes: Italian classic Olio e Aglio next to chewy Chinese long Noodles tossed with Sichuan Chili Oil. For the big finale, Bianca blends Italian style with Chinese flavour for a one-of-a-kind CombiNation Plate: Kung Pao Pork Osso Bucco over Forbidden Black Rice Risotto... made with shiitake mushrooms and bright green edamame peas.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Turkey, think Silk Road spices. Indonesia, lots of seafood and tropical fruits! Curious and adventurous host Bianca Osbourne is your CombiNATION Plates culinary guide through both food cultures. First, check out Turkish Spice Crusted Lamb Rack with yogurt and blistered peppers. Next, an Indonesian tribute: Crab Leg with Coconut Curry. For the 'common ingredient'? Two takes on tender, savoury eggplant! Turkish Eggplant is braised and served with parsley and feta and the Indonesian take is served with fried garlic and sweet soy. For the CombiNATION Plate? Peanut- chili Sate sauce replaces walnut for Pazili TrancÌ§a, a classically Turkish halibut presentation, also served here with unique, savoury, coconut 'baklava' for a plate no-one has ever seen before!

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: From Canada to Greece, CombiNATION Plates always covers lots of ground! Kicking off, host Bianca Osbourne wows with ubERS-Canadian Roasted Venison Loin, yummy Fiddlehead Pancakes and sweet-tart Cranberry sauce. Next, enjoy a scenic Mediterranean vacation on a plate with her completely new approach to Greek Salad, taken up a notch or two with tasty seared Albacore Tuna! This episode's 'common ingredient' is lamb shank; check out two wildly creative re- inventions; first, of Canadian TourtieÌ€re, and then, Greek Dolmades. For a final recipe, a dessert fit for a lumberjack or a Greek god! Oka Saganaki with McIntosh Apple Butter and Maple Loukoumades; made with nutty Canadian Oka cheese, fried crisp, flambeÌed with Ouzo, then dusted with dried raspberry powder and maple sugar flakes. 'Opa!' meets Oka!

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: In this episode of CombiNATION Plates, host Bianca Osbourne starts in Mexico with her version of popular street snack, 'Esquites', re-invented here as sweet corn flan. Next is Norway, don't miss explosively exciting quick-smoked Arctic Char with Forage, you've NEVER seen anything like this before. The common ingredient is tacos! From Norway, 'Lefse' is a potato-flour 'tortilla' served with fresh berries and uniquely Scandinavian 'Brunost' cheese. Representing Mexico, check out spicy pork and pineapple 'Tacos al Pastor'. The Big Dish is Lamb Pinnekjott with Birch Bark Tamale, lamb ribs are seasoned, birch bark is stuffed with masa and veggies, both are steamed over birch branches and boozy Akvavit, then plated with dill and bright red chili oil.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Join Bianca Osbourne to taste and combine! Fun CombiNATION Plates host Bianca Osbourne starts this episode with an inspired tribute to Georgian cuisine; Beef Tartare with Tkemali (sour plum sauce), marigold powder, and tart, crunchy pickles. Next; feast your eyes on her unique Hong Kong dumpling: plump scallops, pork, and veggies are blended, rolled, steamed, sliced, and then served with Crispy Yu Choy and XO. The common ingredient is rabbit! Georgia offers up Rabbit 'Mtsvadi', grilled and glazed with pomegranate reduction. Hong Kong represents with Sweet and Sour Rabbit, Grilled Pineapple and Red Pepper Coulis. Finally, Georgian Milk Braised meets Chinese/Hong Kong-style Roast Duck with quail egg and cheese stuffed 'Khachapuri Dumpling' and crunchy 'Peking Duck Crumble', and it's a plate no-one has ever seen before!

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: CombiNATION Plates host Bianca Osbourne is always looking for the best food in the world to share. The first stop is South Korea with her Sesame Crusted Tuna 'Yukhoe', a delicious blend of favourite Korean flavours. Next stamp on the passport? Spain! Mouth-watering Duck Breast is seared and served rare with a crispy artichoke heart, membrillo, olives, and sweet-savoury sherry reduction. Beef short ribs are the common ingredient with Catalonian Braised and Romesco to one side and sticky Korean Kalbi-style grilled to the east! For the topper, drool over Bulgogi Octopus simmered and served with saffron-scented Sticky Rice Paella cakes, finished with crispy Paprika-Gochujang battered mussel. Yep, it tastes as good as it sounds!

programme.title: Bonacini's Italy
programme.desc: Chef Michael Bonacini uses the fresh ingredients from rich Tuscan soil to make regional dishes like Spinach Ricotta Gnudi.

programme.desc: Matt and his team hit fitness central and cook up a grilled feast that is high on protein and lean on fat including Salt Crusted Peel and Eat Shrimp; Lime Chipotle Pork Medallions; Mesquite Smoked Turkey with Peach Glaze and a Sweet and Spicy Black Bean Salad.

programme.desc: Matt and the Road Grill team are pitching in to feed volunteers some great grilled chow including Lime Jalapeno Chicken Wings with Raita Dip; Beef Ribs with Vinegar Bbq Sauce; Killer Bbq Fries with Homemade Ketchup and Some Ice Cold Kiwi Melon Frostys

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt and the Team join some friends at a Cottage Party to celebrate the end of school. They keep the festivities going with Bbqed Rib Eye Steaks with a Chimmi Churri Sauce; Jerk Pulled Pork Sandwiches with Fresh Mango; Roasted Garlic Onion Potatoes and Greek Salad in a Pita.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Not only do the racers in the Soap Box Derby have a need for speed, they have a need for BBQ! Matt and the RG team cook up a feast to slow down any speedster. The menu: Smoked Shrimp B.L.T., Pineapple Basil Rotisserie Chicken, Grilled Sweet Potato Pops, and Summer Berry Pudding.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Matt spends the day by the water; fishing, playing Frisbee, catching some rays. What goes with water and sun? BBQ!!! Matt & his team make a seafood buffet of Grilled Clams in the Shell with Serrano Ham, Smoked Lake Rainbow Trout, Grilled Lobster with Grapefruit Salsa, and Crab-Stuffed Avocados.

programme.desc: Forget bicycle built for two. This Big Bike fits 30 people! Matt & the team join forces with the Heart & Stroke foundation to raise money by riding around a track. Matt feeds these 'bikers' some healthy and delicious grub. The menu: Grilled Calamari, Smoked Turkey Fajitas, Grilled Scallops and Watermelon Salsa, and Cold Gazpacho.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: It's a bird, it's a plane, it's a day of great BBQ. Inspired by the aerial acrobatics of CF18's and Harvards, Matt cooks up a variety of poultry and fowl. The menu: Bacon Wrapped Quail Stuffed with 3 Cream Cheeses, Raspberry Chipotle Turkey Wings, Grilled Balsamic Marinated Duck Breast, and Deviled Eggs.

programme.title: Road Grill
programme.desc: Whether you're a pro or a beginner, everyone is welcome to join the Road Grill team at the 19th tee where Matt is busy burnin' up his Q's. What better way to end your day at the links with an enticing menu of Miso Citrus Grilled Sablefish, Smoked Rack of Pork with a Sour Cherry Sauce , Lemon Basil Smashed Potatoes with Mushrooms, Arugula Salad with Peaches, and Blue Cheese and Walnuts.

programme.title: Road Grill

The menu: BBQ'd Pork Tenderloin & Pears on Bagels, Grilled Asian Chicken Burger, Surf & Turf Kabobs with Arugula Pesto, and BBQ'd Cinnamon Donuts.

programme.desc: The Road Grill team goes rural and gets lost in hedge mazes at Saunders Farm. Luckily, they find their way out in time to cook up some BBQ and feed all families at the farm. programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Grilling expert Spencer Watts hosts a tasty beef-a-palooza with four incredible beef cuts including smoked brisket and succulent beef tenderloin.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Barbeque is not just about meat! Chef Spencer Watts shows us how to grill fruits and vegetables like a pro.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Charismatic host Spencer Watts shows us why chicken is best on the grill with four irresistible chicken recipes.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: There are limitless options with lamb on the grill! Host Spencer Watts offers some tips and tricks on how to prepare four different cuts of lamb.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Whether for a small family dinner or a big backyard barbeque, host Spencer Watts shows us the best ways to grill up tasty duck, turkey, capon, and quail.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Never underestimate the power of a good side or dessert! Fun host Spencer Watts grills up the perfect sides and treats for your next barbeque.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Pork and the grill are good buddies and host Spencer Watts shows us four of his favourite pork recipes that will have you salivating.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Fun and quirky host Chef Spencer Watts kicks off this episode by showing off his knife skills as he sabres a pineapple to start his instant classic; Grilled Fruit Salad. The pineapple joins a mix of bright summer fruits on the grill, then berries, spicy citrus vinaigrette and savoury feta cheese. Next up, Spencer whips up his Hawaiian Chicken recipe. Spiced with cayenne and Chinese five spice, Spencer stuffs flavourful chicken thighs with pineapple and grills it with a delightful ginger and soy sauce. Every bite is sweet, savoury, and fresh - exactly how summer should taste! Then Spencer shares his top tips for adding smoky flavours to pork using an easy and handy smoke pouch. His Apple Smoked Pork Tenderloin is bathed in a fog of applewood smoke and he then tops it with tart apple-blueberry compote. Cinnamon buns are amazing with a bit of orange peel - but have you ever had them in an orange peel and on the grill? That's right and Spencer shows you how you can make fun and flavourful Orange Peel Cinnamon Buns on the barbecue using indirect heat.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Onions, green onions, chives, garlic, and leeks are all members of the same fabulous flowering family, Allium! First up? Chef Watts shows us his sweet and smoky Charred Leeks with Romesco; add grilled toast and you have your next party appetizer! Next up is a main course. Spencer starts with a juicy grilled pork chop, then adds roasted garlic melted into a balsamic onion chutney to make his out-of-this-world Roasted Garlic Pork Chops. Looking for a crowd pleaser that's easy to make? Spencer has a recipe for that! Chicken Yakitori. He threads skewers with crunchy green onions and tender chicken and caramelizes the yakitori sauce as he grills this tasty dish. Are you ready to drool? Spencer shows us how to make Allium your main dish with Stuffed Vidalia Onions. Loaded with savoury bacon, salty blue cheese and garlic, you'll want to eat this dish for every meal!

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Have a cheese lover coming over? Blow their minds with some of Spencer's "grate" barbecued cheese recipes! First up, Spencer chars Brie Cheese to an ooey-gooey perfection and tops it with tart cherries and crunchy walnuts, serving it with crispy grilled crostinis. Next up is a Grilled Steak and Caesar with a twist only Spencer could add - grilled... lettuce! Then, Spencer uses the classic German "hasselback" presentation to stuff chicken breasts with verdant spinach, yummy Swiss cheese and pungent sun- dried tomatoes, grilling them to melty perfection. Finally, Spencer fires up his barbecue to make his Grilled Peaches with Salted Bourbon Caramel, a decadent dessert elevated with creamy Mascarpone and perfectly balances sweet, smoky, and salty flavours.

programme.title: Maddie and Kiki's BBQ Barn
programme.desc: Fun-loving sisters and expert grillmasters Maddie and Kiki welcome you into their country barn for craveable eats and spicy jokes! On this episode, they smoke salmon to turn it into pastrami, whip up a colorful Caribbean salad, and grill savory rosemary-seasoned pork chops!

programme.title: Watts On the Grill

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Fun and quirky host Chef Spencer Watts kicks off this episode by showing off his knife skills as he sabres a pineapple to start his instant classic; Grilled Fruit Salad. The pineapple joins a mix of bright summer fruits on the grill, then berries, spicy citrus vinaigrette and savoury feta cheese. Next up, Spencer whips up his Hawaiian Chicken recipe. Spiced with cayenne and Chinese five spice, Spencer stuffs flavourful chicken thighs with pineapple and grills it with a delightful ginger and soy sauce. Every bite is sweet, savoury, and fresh - exactly how summer should taste! Then Spencer shares his top tips for adding smoky flavours to pork using an easy and handy smoke pouch. His Apple Smoked Pork Tenderloin is bathed in a fog of applewood smoke and he then tops it with tart apple-blueberry compote. Cinnamon buns are amazing with a bit of orange peel - but have you ever had them in an orange peel and on the grill? That's right and Spencer shows you how you can make fun and flavourful Orange Peel Cinnamon Buns on the barbecue using indirect heat.

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Onions, green onions, chives, garlic, and leeks are all members of the same fabulous flowering family, Allium! First up? Chef Watts shows us his sweet and smoky Charred Leeks with Romesco; add grilled toast and you have your next party appetizer! Next up is a main course. Spencer starts with a juicy grilled pork chop, then adds roasted garlic melted into a balsamic onion chutney to make his out-of-this-world Roasted Garlic Pork Chops. Looking for a crowd pleaser that's easy to make? Spencer has a recipe for that! Chicken Yakitori. He threads skewers with crunchy green onions and tender chicken and caramelizes the yakitori sauce as he grills this tasty dish. Are you ready to drool? Spencer shows us how to make Allium your main dish with Stuffed Vidalia Onions. Loaded with savoury bacon, salty blue cheese and garlic, you'll want to eat this dish for every meal!

programme.title: Watts On the Grill
programme.desc: Have a cheese lover coming over? Blow their minds with some of Spencer's "grate" barbecued cheese recipes! First up, Spencer chars Brie Cheese to an ooey-gooey perfection and tops it with tart cherries and crunchy walnuts, serving it with crispy grilled crostinis. Next up is a Grilled Steak and Caesar with a twist only Spencer could add - grilled... lettuce! Then, Spencer uses the classic German "hasselback" presentation to stuff chicken breasts with verdant spinach, yummy Swiss cheese and pungent sun- dried tomatoes, grilling them to melty perfection. Finally, Spencer fires up his barbecue to make his Grilled Peaches with Salted Bourbon Caramel, a decadent dessert elevated with creamy Mascarpone and perfectly balances sweet, smoky, and salty flavours.

programme.title: Gatra Beach: Indonesia
programme.desc: Gatra Beach conservation is one of the remote destinations in nature that has much to offer.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: Taking flight over Oahu.

programme.title: Flying Over Scotland
programme.desc: Soaring over the lush green landscape and enchanting waters of Scotland in a bird's-eye view voyage.

programme.title: Four Seasons Around the World
programme.desc: A kaleidoscopic journey through a shimmering summer, a cozy fall, a breathtaking winter and a glowing spring.

programme.title: Senaru Waterfalls: Indonesia
programme.desc: Discovering the beauty of waterfalls cascading from a sheer cliff, nestled in lush vegetation.

programme.title: Rainbow Reef Relaxation 2
programme.desc: Vibrant underwater reefs, including many scenes which no longer look as depicted due to coral bleaching.

programme.title: Beautiful Backcountry: Yukon River
programme.desc: The Yukon River snakes a path through the evergreen wilderness.

programme.title: A Day in Santorini
programme.desc: A visual journey through the coastal village of Oia, found on the far tip of Santorini Island.

programme.title: California Waterfall Relaxation
programme.desc: Filmed in Yosemite and Sequoia/Kings Canyon National Parks and Big Sur; relaxing next to places like Yosemite Falls, McWay Falls, Bridal Veil Falls, and many more iconic falls.

programme.title: Patagonia Mountain Stream
programme.desc: A rejuvenating, refreshing window to the glacial meltwater springs of Chile's Torres Del Paine National Park in Patagonia with this 1-hour long fixed-angle nature relaxation video complete with stereo nature sounds of the creek and birds chirping.

programme.title: Atuh Beach: Indonesia
programme.desc: Take a tour of beautiful Indonesian waters, surrounded by impressive rock formations.

programme.title: Turtle Paradise
programme.desc: Dive into an undersea world inhabited by the sea turtles; featuring real-time video scenes captured from around the Caribbean, paired with gentle underwater sounds and calming music for relaxation.

programme.title: Mount Cook: New Zealand
programme.desc: Taking in the rugged beauty of snow-capped mountains and calm emerald waters.

programme.title: The Tranquil Stream
programme.desc: Take in the peaceful, natural beauty of a Pennsylvania stream. Relax as the melodic water flows downstream cradled in the vibrant tree-covered canopy.

programme.title: Gisborne & Lake Twizel: New Zealand
programme.desc: Taking in Gisborne's tranquil cliff-lined coastlines, driftwood, and rushing waves; going lakeside in the Twizel region to explore snow-covered mountain ranges and the calm surface of the lake waters.

programme.title: Above & Under
programme.desc: Flying above beachy shores; diving deep into crystal-clear waters brimming with sea creatures.

programme.title: Ruby Beach Sunset
programme.desc: Relax for a full hour as a golden, soothing sunset slowly unfolds and turns to dusk as seen from Ruby Beach, on Washington state's Olympic Peninsula; you'll be lulled into a state of tranquil relaxation thanks to the sounds of rhythmic ocean waves gently crashing over the pebbly beach paired with the dazzling collage of four different sunset scenes shot in 4K.

programme.title: Tutoko River: New Zealand
programme.desc: Rushing waters through rocky rapids.

programme.title: Bulog Dos Island
programme.desc: Showcasing a small tropical island encircled by crystal-clear turquoise waters called Bulog Dos Island in the Philippines.

programme.title: Four Seasons: New Zealand
programme.desc: A look at New Zealand from four different angles: summer, fall, winter and spring.

programme.title: Wairere Falls: New Zealand
programme.desc: The Wairere Falls of New Zealand.

programme.title: Nacpan Beach
programme.desc: Travelling to a beautiful stretch of cream-colored sand with swaying coconut palms and clear waters.

programme.title: Cliffside Beach: Indonesia
programme.desc: The thrilling view of a cliff-top lookout of one of Indonesia's most breathtaking spots.

programme.title: Beach Paradise: Hawaii
programme.desc: Spending a restful moment in Maui, a tropical paradise of gleaming white sand beaches and turquoise waters.

programme.title: Hamilton Island Palms
programme.desc: Soaking up the tropical breeze with the stunning palm and coastal vista from the famous Hamilton Island, Australia.

programme.title: Flying Over South Korea
programme.desc: The filmmaker showcases South Korea's natural wonders and urban landscapes by exploring four of the country's most famous destinations: Jeju Island, Seoul, Geoje National Park and Busan.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: An aerial journey to the remote fjords and valleys of eastern Iceland.

programme.title: Kapas Biru Waterfall: Indonesia
programme.desc: Escape to Indonesia with tropical vegetation and a rushing waterfall.

programme.title: The Tranquil Stream
programme.desc: Take in the peaceful, natural beauty of a Pennsylvania stream. Relax as the melodic water flows downstream cradled in the vibrant tree-covered canopy.

programme.title: Tropical Weightlessness
programme.desc: Gently sway in the tropical turquoise waters with a glimpse of blue sky and palm trees.

programme.title: Wildlife Wonders of South America
programme.desc: A sunrise at Salar de Uyuni in Bolivia with flamingo and llama scenes from high altitude volcanic salt lakes; welcoming new life in the Chilean landscapes of San Pedro Atacama; the Torres del Paine National Park in Patagonia.

programme.title: Bryce Canyon Relaxation
programme.desc: Admiring the wonders of one of the world's most breathtaking natural destinations: Bryce Canyon, in Utah.

programme.title: Crystal Blue Waves
programme.desc: The sights and sounds of big waves breaking along a coastal pier on a blue-sky day.

programme.title: Peaceful Forest Walk: Quebec
programme.desc: The heart of the Laurentians.

programme.title: Auckland Beaches: New Zealand
programme.desc: The relaxing vibe of a day at the beach, with a tour of the breathtaking coastlines near Auckland.

programme.title: Above & Below the Caribbean
programme.desc: A static scene of Curaçao, Caribbean; featuring swimming fish, passing clouds, and sunshine glistening; filmed by Tilo and Yvonne Kühnast.

programme.title: Relaxing Nature Sceneries
programme.desc: The filmmaker provides a collection of peaceful nature scenes and tranquil vistas that will soothe body and soul.

programme.title: Tranquil Waters: Diablo River
programme.desc: A majestic view of the Diablo River which is surrounded by evergreen mountains.

programme.title: Glistening Island Beach
programme.desc: A big palm tree facing a white sandy beach, glistening blue sea, sunshine, and a small boat; filmed in 4K Ultra High Definition on the hidden island of Nanuku, where a small atoll is surrounded by an untouched lagoon, miles away from it all.

programme.title: Above the Dolomites
programme.desc: Featuring cinematography by new collaborator Ryan Shirley and synchronized to new healing music by Antonio Resende; offering some views that showcase the Dolomites mountain range.

programme.title: Sparkling Californian Coast -- California Coast, U.S.
programme.desc: Lose yourself in the view of sparkling waves crashing over the California coast.

programme.title: Redwood Forest Rejuvenation
programme.desc: Transporting to the magical redwood forests of Northern California and soaking up the healing energies that only pure nature can provide, with multiple healing scenes from the old-grove forest, paired with real-life stereo nature sounds.

programme.title: Crashing Waves: Hawaii
programme.desc: The energizing sound of waves crashing on the shores of Hawaii's Red Sand Beach.

programme.title: Hawaii Palms + Waves
programme.desc: Featuring a scene of coastal palms, green grass and blue waves in the early morning light and a backdrop of stereo nature sounds; shot on location in Oahu, Hawaii at the Turtle Bay Resort.

programme.title: Whispers of the Wilderness
programme.desc: Immerse yourself in the stillness of plant life through every stage, from flowers on the cusp of blooming to snow-dusted trees-all set to nature's soothing soundtrack.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Florence
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Florence, the heart of Tuscany and birthplace of the Renaissance. She visits iconic landmarks like the Duomo, Uffizi Gallery, and Ponte Vecchio, uncovering the city’s artistic legacy. Along the way, she enjoys Tuscan classics like Bistecca alla Fiorentina and the delicious schiaccata panino.

programme.title: L' ombra lunga del colonialismo
programme.desc: Italian colonial heritage, long confined to museum storage due to its controversial past, is now being re-examined to find new meanings and contemporary interpretations.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Barcelona
programme.desc: Barcelona, Spain, is a city close to Malini's heart. Returning after her student years, she retraces old steps to see how the city has changed. Barcelona, one of Europe’s most visited cities, still holds centuries-old charm. Malina's old haunts include El Gòtic, Sant Antoni, Passeig de Gràcia, and Barceloneta. visits Gaudí’s iconic Casa La Pedrera, and discovers the new wave of creative tapas restaurants in El Raval, a once run-down quarter now bursting with energy.

programme.title: Whats On Waves and Rhythms

Malta Boomerang Festival and Earth Garden celebrate eco conscious music and arts, while Spain Bilbao BBK fuses urban culture with live performances. Set sail on the Mediterranea Metal Cruise, a unique floating festival, and enjoy Malta iconic Jazz Festival with historic and coastal backdrops. The Mediterranean truly comes alive where music meets the waves.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Palermo
programme.desc: Malini Angelica returns to Sicily to explore Palermo, Mondello, and Agrigento. From Palermo’s vibrant markets and rich history to Mondello’s seaside charm and the ancient Greek temples of Agrigento, she uncovers the island’s unique mix of cultures, cuisine, and character that make Sicily truly one of a kind.

programme.title: Med Destinations Crete
programme.desc: Med Destinations takes you to Crete, the jewel of the Greek islands, where ancient history meets breathtaking landscapes. Explore charming villages, hidden beaches, and the vibrant local culture that makes Crete a must-visit Mediterranean paradise.

programme.title: Whats On Conferences, Startups and Innovation
programme.desc: A new world is being built right now across the Mediterranean, and we are taking you behind the scenes of the most important meetings of the year. This week, we are at the historic Mediterranean Conference Centre in Valletta for MedTech World, where the conversation has shifted from simple gadgets to responsible AI that doctors and patients can actually trust. We then head south to the incredible energy of AfricaIgnite to meet the founders of high-growth startups in climate tech and finance who are proving that Africa is the new engine of global innovation. Back on the islands, we explore the Startup Malta Festival to see how a pro-business ecosystem is helping entrepreneurs scale their boldest ideas across Europe. Finally, we join the global dialogue at the Finance Malta Conference to see how a focus on quality over quantity is redefining the future of investment and sustainability. This is a journey through the ideas and the people who are turning today's challenges into the success stories of the future.

programme.title: Med Destinations Delos

programme.title: Verona I della Scala
programme.desc: This episode tells Verona’s story through the rise and fall of the Della Scala family, pioneers of the Italian Renaissance. From Cangrande to Mastino II, we follow their power struggle with Venice, booming trade, and eventual exile to Bavaria.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: Naples is Italy in the extreme. We’ll prowl backstreet fish markets and dodge speedy Vespas before indulging in pizza where it was invented. Afterward, we'll climb to the top of nearby Mount Vesuvius, then wander through the ruins of Pompeii, the Roman town it destroyed.

programme.title: Whats On Conferences, Startups and Innovation
programme.desc: A new world is being built right now across the Mediterranean, and we are taking you behind the scenes of the most important meetings of the year. This week, we are at the historic Mediterranean Conference Centre in Valletta for MedTech World, where the conversation has shifted from simple gadgets to responsible AI that doctors and patients can actually trust. programme.title: Into the Med Gozo and Comino Hidden Gems
programme.desc: The adventure begins in the bright waters of the Blue Lagoon and the Crystal Lagoon. Susannah takes us through these amazing natural pools, showing us the raw beauty of Comino.

programme.title: Mediterranean Yoga Calm Your Body
programme.desc: Mediterranean Yoga is a series that blends Embodiment Yoga with the peaceful beauty of the Mediterranean. Set in stunning coastal and natural locations, each episode encourages viewers to reconnect mind and body through gentle movement, breathwork, and mindfulness in a safe and calming space.

programme.title: Acqua da proteggere
programme.desc: With Marco Faimali, director of the CNR's Institute for the Study of Marine Sustainability, we explore the ocean's fragile ecosystem and the need for protection. Anna Pancaldi visits marine protected areas, including the Smart Bay of Santa Teresa and the port of La Spezia, where researchers study "alien species". Finally, we learn about the underwater internet, an innovation from Sapienza University of Rome led by Professor Chiara Petrioli and the WSENSE team.

programme.title: Dates that made History
programme.desc: 399 BC: The Trial of Socrates dramatises the famous trial of the Greek philosopher Socrates, who is charged with corrupting the youth and impiety. The episode delves into Socrates' defence, his philosophical ideas, and his unwavering commitment to his principles. It explores the tension between individual thought and the societal norms of ancient Athens. Through this trial, the show examines the impact of Socrates' ideas on Western philosophy and his legacy.

programme.title: The Great Greek Myths
programme.desc: The tale of Tartarus: The Damned of the Earth descends into the deepest abyss of the Greek underworld, where the most cursed souls are imprisoned. Here dwell legendary figures punished for defying the gods, bound by eternal torment. This dark realm serves as a chilling reminder of divine justice and the price of hubris. A myth that explores punishment, morality, and the shadow side of eternity.

programme.title: Macbeth Part 1
programme.desc: Macbeth Part 1

programme.title: OSN Kubrick - Musica e cinema d'autore
programme.desc: From the Rai Arturo Toscanini Auditorium in Turin, the Rai National Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Min Chung, presents a symphonic concert dedicated to the iconic soundtracks from Stanley Kubrick's films. The programme features orchestral interpretations of memorable scores that have shaped Kubrick's cinematic legacy, blending classical masterpieces with evocative film music for a powerful and immersive experience.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Sevilla
programme.desc: Malini Angelica explores Seville, Spain — a vibrant city in the heart of Andalusia known for its rich Moorish heritage, flamenco music, and stunning architecture. She visits iconic landmarks like the Seville Cathedral, Plaza de España, and the Royal Alcázar, while also discovering local tapas bars and lively streets filled with culture and charm.

programme.title: Whats On Books Literature Innovation and Learning
programme.desc: Great ideas are the lifeblood of the Mediterranean and this week we are following them from the written page to the boardroom. We start at the Malta Book Festival, where the local community gathers to celebrate the stories that define the islands. Our journey then takes us to Cyprus for the Nicosia Book Fest to see how literature creates a unique bridge between cultures. In Spain, we wander the famous leafy avenues of the Madrid Book Fair, where Retiro Park becomes one of the largest outdoor libraries in the world. We wrap up at the historic Mediterranean Conference Centre in Valletta for the EU Startup Summit, where the creative energy of storytelling meets the high-stakes world of global innovation. This is a look at the parks and the halls where the next chapter of the Mediterranean is being written.

programme.title: Malini Angelica Athens
programme.desc: Malini explores the ancient city of Athens, Greece. Once steeped in history, Athens has experienced a modern rebirth, now thriving with creativity, modern Greek gastronomy, a vibrant contemporary art scene, and unmatched nightlife.

programme.title: Med Destinations Crete
programme.desc: Med Destinations takes you to Crete, the jewel of the Greek islands, where ancient history meets breathtaking landscapes. Explore charming villages, hidden beaches, and the vibrant local culture that makes Crete a must-visit Mediterranean paradise.

programme.title: Colours of Mediterranean Culture
programme.desc: From Malta to Greece, Mediterranean arts flourish. Valletta Baroque Festival, Malta International Arts Festival, and Birgu by Candlelight light up the island with music and heritage. Italy’s Cortona on the Move, La Fenice in Piazza San Marco, and Mercantia Festival showcase photography, opera, and street performance. France’s Art Paris and International Garden Festival, Spain’s Revela’t Festival and La Leyenda de Tartessos, Portugal’s Umbrella Sky Project and Walk & Talk Biennale, Turkey’s Istanbul Theatre Festival and Biennial, and Greece’s Athens & Epidaurus Festival highlight the Med’s rich artistic tapestry.

programme.title: Rick Steves Europe
programme.desc: We'll explore flamboyant Sevilla, where orange trees ring lacy palaces, bullfighting is still a cultural event, and little girls dream of growing up to become flamenco dancers.

programme.title: Med Nations 04 France
programme.desc: France, where the sun shines on the Mediterranean, and there is a lot of culture and style. Along the beautiful French Riviera, from Nice to Marseille, France shows off a lively mix of history, food, and coastal life that has shaped the Mediterranean's spirit.

This powerful documentary reveals stunning images of the sea’s beauty and the urgent struggle to protect it, offering a moving portrait of our relationship with nature.

programme.desc: Med Destinations takes you to Crete, the jewel of the Greek islands, where ancient history meets breathtaking landscapes. Explore charming villages, hidden beaches, and the vibrant local culture that makes Crete a must-visit Mediterranean paradise.

programme.title: Mediterranean Kitchen France
programme.desc: Chef Kurt invites Chef Geoffrey into the kitchen to cook a brilliant edible art show: delicate French pastries intricately designed to mimic Malta’s iconic architecture. As they sculpt and bake, the conversation centres on precision, the discipline of pastry work, and how travel inspires creative fusion. It’s a visually stunning masterclass that proves food can be a bridge between two cultures.

programme.title: Med Destinations Rimini
programme.desc: Med Destinations visits Rimini, Italy’s seaside gem, where golden beaches meet rich history. From Roman ruins to vibrant nightlife, Rimini offers the perfect mix of culture and coastal charm.

programme.title: Io Ti Cerchero
programme.desc: The investigation into Ettore's death reaches a critical juncture. The authorities have apprehended two suspects, and their convictions seem imminent. However, Valerio, Sara, the prosecutor, and Ettore's friends remain unconvinced of their guilt. They continue to harbour doubts, believing that the true circumstances of Ettore's death have yet to be uncovered.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.desc: On a visit to Hawai, the land of the Mishmi tribe, Roshni stays with some members of the tribe, who treat her to a rather potent local brew.

programme.title: Viajar Peru
programme.desc: In the last leg of her journey, Milagros goes to Trujillo, where she introduces the flavors of the northern region of Peru and also Marinera – the sensuous national dance. But the highlight in Trujillo are the forgotten sites like Chan Chan and Huaca del Moche, where secrets from Peru’s pre-Incan past begin to unfold.

programme.title: City Break
programme.desc: Take a break from your mundane routine and set out to discover what different cities have to offer with City Breaks.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Quest
programme.desc: From chicken with bamboo shoots to a banana stem dish called Posola Musar Ali Khan, Alex learns to make some popular dishes of Assam.

programme.title: Xplore Austria
programme.desc: Elisha is starstruck by the musical heritage of Salzburg, which was the setting for Mozart’s compositions and the story of the Von Trapp family.

programme.title: Unplanned Trip
programme.desc: On a visit to Hawai, the land of the Mishmi tribe, Roshni stays with some members of the tribe, who treat her to a rather potent local brew.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s adventures in Perth kick off with him immersing himself in the historic grandeur of the Scone Castle, where a mysterious stone tells tales of Scottish monarchs. But the real thunder of the Highlands awaits at the Braemar Games gathering, where tartan meets brawn, and Robbie learns that embracing his Scottish side involves more than just kilts and castles.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Novi Sad, the financial and industrial hub of Serbia.Starting with canoeing on Danube and later exploring the city and its landmarks.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Santa Village at Rovaniemi, where she reads some of the letters that children from around the world write to him.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Luxurious hotels, thrilling water parks, inexpensive food and almost 300 days of bright sunny weather make Bulgaria’s biggest seaside resort, Sunny Beach popular for budget beach getaways. Catriona, however, stumbles onto the historic town of Nessebar, which is over 3000 years old, and realizes that there is more to the place than meets the eye. She goes on to visit the Castra Rubra winery, where she finds out about the history of Viticulture in Bulgaria and the process of wine making.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From exploring a vintage car museum to scaling a 5 meter ice-wall and sampling a rich bread cookie, Watch Alex exploring The Hague, also known as the “International city of peace”.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: A modern European oil city built on the back of a rich maritime heritage, Aberdeen is the energetic kingpin of northeast Scotland. While the boom times may come and go, the dynamic coastal metropolis never ceases to thrill with its free museums, outdoor adventures and natural attractions right on the city’s doorstep.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Heather explores Požarevac and visits the ancient town of Viminacium. Later visits National Museum, concluding with Majdanpek’s Rajkova Pecina Caves.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline Nsingi Nkosi goes to Rovaniemi, where she visits a husky farm and finds out about about breeding and raising huskies.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: For the people of Sofia, preserving its heritage is sacrosanct, and this can be seen in the way the city's infrastructure is built around its archeological treasures. Catriona realizes this when she roams around the cobblestone streets of the city. With every monument she visits, she finds herself transported to a different era, right from Perperikon fort, which narrate stories of the Byzantine empire, to Boyana church, which is home to some of the earliest examples of medieval artwork.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: From lazing in hammocks by the beach to sipping drinks on a private yacht, Kim realizes that Maldives is the perfect destination to unwind in style.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying dishes like Stroopwafel and Palat Stoofvlees to visiting the Erasmus bridge, cube houses and wall painting on the streets, Alex explores Rotterdam.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Since medieval times Aberdeenshire and its northwestern neighbour Moray have been the richest and most fertile regions of the Highlands. Aberdeenshire is famed for its Aberdeen Angus beef cattle, its many fine castles and the prosperous 'granite city' of Aberdeen.

programme.title: Backpack Serbia
programme.desc: Explore Majdanpek with our host as the city brings the viewers closer to the treasures strewn across the Serbian countryside.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Levi is Finland's largest and fastest-growing ski destination. Emeline is here, to find out the exciting activities that Levi offers, in summer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: With snow-capped peaks rising over primeval forests and traditional villages, Paro is like an Oriental Switzerland. On a visit to Paro, Radhika explores famous monasteries such as the Rinpung Dzong and Takstang Monastery, and learns the history and legends associated with them. Along the way, she finds out the secrets to the quintessential Bhutanese peace and contentment.

programme.title: Great World Hotels
programme.desc: An invigorating spa treatment, a cooking lesson, a snorkeling session – Kim has experiences in the Maldives that leave her completely rejuvenated.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: From trying Orange zest beer and traditional Dutch dishes to exploring St.Martin's Cathedral, Dom Tower and enjoying go karting and stand up paddle boat ride, watch Alex enjoy his trip in Utrecht.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: In the enchanting landscapes of Inverness, Robbie’s journey takes a whimsical turn: from eccentric to downright magical.

programme.title: Boarding Pass
programme.desc: Emeline goes to Ruka-Kuusamo, where she tries river rafting. She also meets a reindeer herder,who tells her about the unique ways of herding reindeer.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Actress and travel enthusiast, Radhika Bangia explores the enigma that is Bhutan, when she visits Thimpu, the capital city with no traffic lights and no skyscrapers invading the skyline. Here, she checks out significant sights such as the Tibetan-style National Memorial Chorten, the National Library and the Folk Heritage Museum in order to understand more about the beliefs, history, architecture and cuisine of Bhutan.

programme.title: World Heritage
programme.desc: Explore the ancient heritage sites of Abu Simbel, the Philae complex of Nubia and the Hatshepsut Temple to unravel the mystical stories behind the ancient temples of Egypt.

programme.desc: Alex tries traditional Dutch food in Leiden, learns about Dutch history at Prinsenhof Museum, visits a Gothic church, a local cheese market and Delftware factory at Gouda.

programme.desc: The grand finale of Robbie’s wild homecoming unfolds amidst the rugged beauty of the Isle of Skye.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: Krisann and Nathan’s vacation in Mauritius begins in Le Morne, where an unexpected adventure awaits the latter.

programme.title: Xplore Bhutan
programme.desc: Known as Druk Yul or the “Land of the Thunder Dragon”, Bhutan is the country of mountains, monasteries and mystery. Radhika Bangia finds this to be true when she visits Punakha, the former capital of Bhutan, where she marvels at the architecture of Punakha Dzong and gets to know the story behind the two rivers, Mo Chhu and Po Chhu. She also explores the sacred temples and Mebar Tsho lake of Bumthang, the religious heart of Bhutan, and gets to know the local legends.

programme.desc: Jamie Srubis tries out spa treatments, which are inspired by the Dead Sea and the desert surroundings at Hotel Kempinski Ishtar in Jordan.

programme.desc: In her final leg of journey, Heather visits the Uvac canyon, tries locally prepared pie, ending with Kopaonik National Park and Jelovarnik waterfalls.

programme.title: With Love From Mauritius
programme.desc: On an island-hopping adventure in Mauritius, Krisann and Nathan find out that time works differently on these islands.

programme.title: Food Fact Fun
programme.desc: In Amsterdam, Alex tries Bitterballen, cannabis cake and visits Heineken Experience brewery. He explores Zaanse Schans village with windmills and Coster Diamonds, the oldest diamond polishing factory.

programme.title: 10 Days Scotland
programme.desc: Robbie’s journey to reconnect with his Scottish roots begins in Edinburgh, the stunning capital city perched on extinct volcanoes. Navigating the twists of the Royal Mile, strutting around the majestic Edinburgh castle and learning to sip scotch like a true Scotsman, he feels a tad closer to his heritage. The real fun kicks in, however, when he stumbles onto a rather flamboyant way to unleash his inner Highlander.

programme.title: Xplore Bulgaria
programme.desc: Like Rome, Plovdiv straddles seven hills; but as Europe’s oldest continuously inhabited city, it’s far more ancient. As Catriona takes a tour of the city to see its iconic attractions such as a Roman Amphitheatre, which was once the setting for gladiator fights, and the striking Baroque Saints Constantine and Helena Church, she begins to see how seamlessly modern life blends with the age-old ruins. Along the way, she realizes why Plovdiv is called the ‘Eternal City’ of Bulgaria.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: In questa puntata, Ornella Fado conduce un favoloso evento dedicato alla Regione Calabria da Eataly Downtown, preparando ricette insieme allo chef calabrese Luca Caligiuro e allo chef italo-americano Ryan Rizzuto.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Ornella Fado è a Milano, all'EXPO, con i giovani della Basilicata del progetto Sano è Lucano. I prodotti tipici lucani ispirano una serie di incontri culinari: dalla barca alla Mondadori, fino a Lady Bu Bistrò e al ristorante stellato Il Luogo di Aimo e Nadia con lo chef Fabio Pisani.Il racconto è arricchito dagli interventi di Sara Farnetti e Pietro Bitonti.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: In questa puntata, Ornella Fado conduce un favoloso evento dedicato alla Regione Calabria da Eataly Downtown, preparando ricette insieme allo chef calabrese Luca Caligiuro e allo chef italo-americano Ryan Rizzuto.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Emanuela Rossi, famosa attrice e doppiatrice italiana che ha dato voce a molte grandi attrici di Hollywood, da Michelle Pfeiffer a Cate Blanchett.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: cono di stesa, venere nera, e babà al bergamotto. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: portafoglio di squacquerone, ciliegie e mortadella, riso rosso integrale con pollo e peperoni, diplomatico alle rose. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: rotolo di riso, prosciutto e melone, risotto alla rucola con kiwi e vongole, e budino di riso. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Terza ed ultima puntata della serie. Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici sono pronti: chi sceglieranno per volare a NEW YORK per il prestigioso festival della danza? Ancora una volta i ragazzi devono convincere l'importante giuria con il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Parson" con la maestra Abby Silva.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Nella prima puntata Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici incontrano il primo gruppo di ballerini.Per essere selezionati per il prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK devono affrontare 3 sfide: presentare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Graham" con il maestro Antonio Fini.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: La puntata di oggi è ricca di contenuti. Ornella Fado incontra Cristina Carrani, esperta in materia immobiliare che aiuterà Ornella a scoprire l'Isola di Capri. Dalla moda alla storia, al cibo e all'arte, Brindiamo! Edizione speciale Capri è l'episodio perfetto per festeggiare i 18 anni di Brindiamo! su NYC LIFE.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. I suoi amici chef in questa puntata son lo chef Raffaele Ronca, lo Chef Marco Chirico e il giovane pizzaiolo Ettore Toches. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen la musica è dal vivo con la soprano Cristina Fontanelli ed il compositore Jhon La Barbera, tra gli ospiti di questa puntata il Fashion designer Domenico Vacca. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado continua il suo giro per incontrare il suoi amici ristoratori durante le festività di capodanno. Visitando a midtown Chef Pino alla "La Masseria", chef Stefano e Franco a " Viceversa", a "PizzArte" incontra il pizzaiolo Roberto ed il proprietario Dario, a Queens visita "Ornella Trattoria" dove incontra Vincenzo e "Trattoria l'Incontro"dove incontra lo chef e proprietario Rocco. (2013/2014)

programme.title: BrindiamoS3
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita Rolando Biamonte ad una delle sue pizzerie, Numero 28, dove oltre a preparare delle deliziose pizze, con la Signora Eugenia, la mamma di Rolando, Ornella prepara alcune ricette della sua amata terra calabrese. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita "Eataly", New York e con la chef Alicia Walter, prepara delle ricette con i prodotti tipici calabresi. Ornella incontra ed intervsita Dino Borri uno dei fondatori e proprietari di Eataly ed alcuni suoi amici calabresi che vivono e lavorano a New York.(2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova a Milano esattamente all'EXPO. Incontra un gruppo di ragazzi della regione Basilicata, che partecipano al progetto "Sano è Lucano", e hanno portato alcuni prodotti tipici della regione Basilicata, che Ornella utilizza per cucinare alcune ricette con Jasmin sulla barca, con Antonio alla libreria Mondadori di Milano, con Nicolò a "Lady Bu Bistrò", e con Fabio Pisani al ristorante stellato di Aimo e Nadia.(2015)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova in una delle città più belle al mondo, Venezia, e visita il "Centurion Palace", dove incontra lo chef veneziano Massimo Livan al ristorante "Antinoo's Lounge". Con chef Massimo, Ornella prepara dei piatti ispirati ai dipinti d' arte moderna. Durante la visita al "Centurion Palace" incontra il General Manager Paolo Morra, e davanti ad un cocktail tutto veneziano Paolo racconta la storia di questo storico palazzo. (2015)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova in Florida ospite di un eccezionale evento organizzato da Ursula Avella Presidente di "Great Culinary Minds". L 'evento si è svolto al ristorante "Valentino Cucina Italiana". Ospite di Ornella è il talentuoso chef Mauro Uliassi, all'epoca 2 stelle Michelin, ora 3. Ospite d'eccezione di questa puntata il cantautore Raf che incontra Ornella su una spiaggia di Miami. (2015)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova a Brooklyn e visita il ristorante di Joe e chef Marco Chirico per preparare alcune specialità del ristorante. Prima di andare in cucina, Chef Marco preprare alcuni cocktail creati da lui, immaginate che chef Marco prepara i suoi liquori, come il limoncello e il mandarincello. La Happy hours al ristorante di Marco Polo è una combinazione di delizie tipiche italiane, formaggi e salumi ed una presentazione di frutti di mare, ostriche e gamberi.(2015)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado prosegue il suo viaggio culinario nel ristorante Enoteca, il ristorante si trova affianco a Marco Polo. Enoteca è il regno di Chef Marco Chirico, ed e` propio ad Enoteca che chef Marco prepara piatti sempre più originali come il Vulcano Pizza e il Cannolo Pizza (2015)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado visita cinque amici ristoratori durante le feste Natalizie, prima tappa" Angelina's" a Staten Island, poi e` la volta di "Marco Polo" e "Luzzo" a Brooklyn. A Manhattan nell'Upper East side, Ornella visita "83 e mezza". Ultima tappa per ujn buon gelato,"Dolce Gelateria" nel Village. (2013)

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Nella prima puntata Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici incontrano il primo gruppo di ballerini.Per essere selezionati per il prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK devono affrontare 3 sfide: presentare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Graham" con il maestro Antonio Fini.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Continuano le audizioni di Antonio Fini e dei suoi giudici per selezionare i migliori danzatori da invitare al prestigioso festival di danza in NEW YORK. I ragazzi affrontano le tre sfide previste: portare il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di Hip hop con Mirco Giordano e Marcello Gargarelli.

programme.title: Change your Destiny
programme.desc: Terza ed ultima puntata della serie. Antonio Fini ed i suoi giudici sono pronti: chi sceglieranno per volare a NEW YORK per il prestigioso festival della danza? Ancora una volta i ragazzi devono convincere l'importante giuria con il loro pezzo migliore, la contemporary battle e la lezione di danza "tecnica Parson" con la maestra Abby Silva.

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: rotolo di riso, prosciutto e melone, risotto alla rucola con kiwi e vongole, e budino di riso. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: Il CIRCOLO DEL BUONGUSTAI Con Fabio Campoli
programme.desc: Benvenuti a questo nuovo episodio del Circolo dei Buongustai! Cosa bolle in pentola? Oggi il nostro chef Fabio Campoli ci delizierà con tre ricette speciali: cono di stesa, venere nera, e babà al bergamotto. Buona visione a tutti!

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado, passa una giornata a New York con il suo amico Geniale, il Maestro Antonio Ciacca, e tra una chiacchierata a Lincoln Center ed una passeggiata a Central Park, arrivano al prestigiosto Pier Hotel dove il Maestro Ciacca si esibisce regolarmente. in questo episodio di "MY TALENTED FRIENDS" scoprirete la storia di successo del Maestro Ciacca in un pccolo documentario MY JAZZ LIFE.

programme.title: My Talented Friends Season 1
programme.desc: Ornella Fado introduce il suo amico Geniale Julian Gargiulo, un talentuosissimo compositore e pianista on uno spiccato senso dell'umore, Julian e` il protagnosita e regista di un documentario girato al Carnegie Hall di New York, durante una competizione ideada dal maestro Gargiiulo.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Franco Ricci, Presidente Fondazione Italiana Sommelier. Franco racconta l'importanza della cultura del vino in Italia e come la sua fondazione ha rivoluzionato questo settore in oltre 20 anni.

programme.title: Eccellenze Italiane con Piero Muscari intervista a Heinz Beck
programme.desc: Piero Muscari intervista Emanuela Rossi, famosa attrice e doppiatrice italiana che ha dato voce a molte grandi attrici di Hollywood, da Michelle Pfeiffer a Cate Blanchett.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita chef Cesare Casella nel suo ristorante nel Village "Maremma" per preparare alcune delle sue ricette preferite. Con Cesare Ornella visita upstate un allevamento di maiali e mucche Chianine. Cesare Casella oltre ad essere un bravissimo chef è anche uno stimatissimo insegnate, è infatti il Dean del programma di cucina italiana all' "International Culinary Center" a Soho ed Ornella è andata a trovarlo con le telecamere di Brindiamo!.

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante "Il Circo" nel cuore di Manhattan, dove conosce la signora Egidiana autrice di diversi libri di cucina. Dopo aver preprato alcune specialità del ristorante, Ornella visita il Re della ristorazione francese al ristornate Le Cirque. Il Signore Mauro Maccioni in una intervsita racconta ad Ornella la sua favolosa carriera nella ristorazione.(2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante toscano "Pepolino" dove prepara delle saporite ricette con lo chef Patrizio, per chiudere in "Dolcezza e Bellezza" Enzo prepara una delle cheese cake piu` famose di New York.(2007).

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita uno dei ristoranti più eleganti di Manhattan a due passi da Central Park il "San Domenico ristorante". Nella cucina del ristorante Ornella assiste Odette Fada nella preparazione dei classici piatti Italiani. Ospite della puntata la giovane stilista Nicole Romano. Ornella Fado indossa alcune creazioni della sua collezione. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York ed invita i suoi amici chef per preparare insieme delle ricette da gustare durante le festività natalizie. In questa puntata Chef Pino Coladonato, Chef Marcello Russodivito e la giovane pizzaiola Giorgia Caporuscio. In questa edizione di Brindiamo! in my Kitchen ospite speciale la soprano Cristina Fontanelli e John La Barbera, oltre a vari ospiti del mondo dello spettacolo, e della moda. (2016)

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado si trova nel laboratorio di Urbani Truffle a New York per la serconda parte di Brindiamo! in my kitchen. programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: La puntata di oggi è ricca di contenuti. Ornella Fado incontra Cristina Carrani, esperta in materia immobiliare che aiuterà Ornella a scoprire l'Isola di Capri. Dalla moda alla storia, al cibo e all'arte, Brindiamo! Edizione speciale Capri è l'episodio perfetto per festeggiare i 18 anni di Brindiamo! su NYC LIFE.

programme.title: Brindiamo! With Ornella Fado
programme.desc: Ornella Fado è ancora in Bermuda, e questa volta oltre a preparare alcuni piatti con l' executive chef del "Griffin's Bistro" Pietro Casalicchio. Ornella visita le meraviglie dell'isola via mare.

programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita una delle più belle scuole di cucine upstate in New York la "CIA" Culinary Institute of America, dove visita il ristorante "Caterina dei Medici", e con lo chef Scapin preprano alcune ricette del menù della scuola. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Raffaele Trovato, direttore dell'IFSE, a Piobesi Torinese, oltre a visitare la bellissima scuola, Ornella prepara delle ricette con l'istruttore del centro.(2009)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Washington DC e va a scoprire un ristorante proprio a due passi dalla Casa Bianca, il ristorante si chiama "Luigino", il proprietario e chef è Carmine Marzano. Chef Marzano prepara delle ricette tipiche piemontesi. Ospite della trasmissione la bellissima Adrianna Sgarlata Miss Virginia 2006. (2006)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita il ristorante "Il Tartufo" a Manayunk, poco distante da Filadelfia, dove incontra Al Del Bello, per preparare alcune ricette tipiche del ristorante. In questa puntata Ornella indossa i guanti da boxer per correre come Rocky Balboa sulla scalinata del museo d'arte di Filadelfia. (2007)

programme.title: Brindiamo!
programme.desc: Questa puntata fa parte della serie vintage. Ornella Fado visita Al Del Bello al suo ristorante "Il Portico", uno dei ristoranti più raffinati di Filadelfia. Al racconta la sua storia di gratidudine nei confronti della famiglia Lattanzi proprietari di ristoratori italiani a New York. (2006)
